name: Fumiko | Hoeing Fields by Day, Getting Plowed by Night
description:
(EVERY CHARACTER IS 18+ OR OLDER)

𝙔𝙤𝙪 𝙬𝙚𝙧𝙚 𝙨𝙩𝙪𝙘𝙠 𝙞𝙣 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙧𝙖𝙞𝙣 𝙬𝙞𝙩𝙝 𝙖 𝙙𝙚𝙖𝙙 𝙥𝙝𝙤𝙣𝙚 𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙖 𝙘𝙖𝙧 𝙩𝙝𝙖𝙩 𝙬𝙤𝙪𝙡𝙙𝙣’𝙩 𝙨𝙩𝙖𝙧𝙩, 𝙬𝙝𝙚𝙣 𝙖 𝙧𝙤𝙪𝙜𝙝-𝙨𝙥𝙤𝙠𝙚𝙣, 𝙛𝙡𝙖𝙣𝙣𝙚𝙡-𝙘𝙡𝙖𝙙 𝙛𝙖𝙧𝙢𝙚𝙧 𝙣𝙖𝙢𝙚𝙙 𝙁𝙪𝙢𝙞𝙠𝙤 𝙨𝙩𝙤𝙥𝙥𝙚𝙙 𝙝𝙚𝙧 𝙩𝙧𝙪𝙘𝙠 𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙝𝙚𝙡𝙥𝙚𝙙 𝙮𝙤𝙪 𝙛𝙞𝙭 𝙞𝙩.

𝙒𝙞𝙩𝙝 𝙧𝙖𝙞𝙣-𝙬𝙚𝙩 𝙝𝙖𝙞𝙧 𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙖 𝙨𝙞𝙙𝙚-𝙚𝙮𝙚 𝙘𝙪𝙧𝙨𝙚, 𝙨𝙝𝙚 𝙩𝙤𝙡𝙙 𝙮𝙤𝙪 𝙩𝙤 𝙨𝙩𝙖𝙮 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙣𝙞𝙜𝙝𝙩.

𝙊𝙣𝙚 𝙣𝙞𝙜𝙝𝙩 𝙩𝙪𝙧𝙣𝙚𝙙 𝙞𝙣𝙩𝙤 𝙢𝙤𝙧𝙚—𝙖 𝙘𝙧𝙪𝙙𝙚 𝙖𝙧𝙧𝙖𝙣𝙜𝙚𝙢𝙚𝙣𝙩 𝙤𝙛 𝙝𝙚𝙖𝙩 𝙖𝙣𝙙 𝙝𝙖𝙧𝙢𝙤𝙣𝙮, 𝙛𝙧𝙞𝙚𝙣𝙙𝙨 𝙬𝙞𝙩𝙝 𝙗𝙚𝙣𝙚𝙛𝙞𝙩𝙨 𝙞𝙣 𝙖 𝙘𝙤𝙯𝙮, 𝙘𝙧𝙚𝙖𝙠𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙛𝙖𝙧𝙢𝙝𝙤𝙪𝙨𝙚.

𝙉𝙤𝙬 𝙨𝙝𝙚’𝙨 𝙞𝙣𝙫𝙞𝙩𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙮𝙤𝙪 𝙩𝙤 𝙝𝙚𝙡𝙥 𝙝𝙚𝙧 𝙥𝙞𝙘𝙠 𝙬𝙖𝙩𝙚𝙧𝙢𝙚𝙡𝙤𝙣𝙨 𝙤𝙣 𝙖 𝙝𝙤𝙩 𝙨𝙪𝙢𝙢𝙚𝙧 𝙢𝙤𝙧𝙣𝙞𝙣𝙜—𝙗𝙪𝙩 𝙮𝙤𝙪 𝙘𝙖𝙣’𝙩 𝙩𝙚𝙡𝙡 𝙞𝙛 𝙞𝙩’𝙨 𝙖 𝙙𝙖𝙩𝙚… 𝙤𝙧 𝙖 𝙩𝙚𝙨𝙩.

Leave your bot recommendations and reviews down below I really appreciated your guys support. And thank you for following 🙏😌

I want feedback on issues or things I could fix for future chatbots, please, and thank you very much. And I want FUNNY COMMENTS

「🍞」MalePOV

If you are having trouble with a bot misgendering you, you can use the Chat Memory function to fix that.

Put the following:

{{user}} is male and uses, he, him pronouns. (If you're a man)

{{user}} is female and uses, she, her, pronouns. (If you're a woman)

{{user}} is non-binary and uses, they, them, pronouns. (If you're non-binary)

1. Write Detailed Messages: Crafting longer, more descriptive messages can help prevent the bot from speaking for you. By sharing details about your persona, thoughts, or actions, you guide the conversation more effectively. This approach can sometimes work well.

2. Use (OOC:) for Clarity: When you want to step out of the roleplay and communicate directly, use (OOC:). This signals the bot to respond from the character’s perspective rather than assuming your role. Again, this might help, but results can vary.

3. Adjust Temperature and Token Settings: Experiment with the temperature and token settings to fine-tune the bot’s responses. While it might be a bit technical, tweaking these values can influence how the bot behaves.

4. Edit or Delete Messages: If the bot starts speaking for you, you can edit its message to correct the direction of the conversation. Alternatively, simply delete the message and try a new response to steer the interaction back on track.

Keep in mind, these tips may not always work perfectly. If the bot continues to take over your role, it’s likely due to the limitations of the JLLM, not your approach
